mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers short-rate discounting and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 prices rise over the next month when the policy rate is falling rather than flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after stable or rising FEDFUNDS in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates enough to force a higher or sustained FEDFUNDS path, real discount rates and recession odds rise, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3 percentage points year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events do not produce negative 60-day forward returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional average by more than 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if rising UNRATE does not coincide with lower 20-day forward returns versus falling UNRATE in rolling out-of-sample tests, or if the sign is positive in more than 40% of quarterly windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in PAYEMS together with a falling UNRATE indicates labor-market strength and wage-supportive demand, which boosts near-term earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the prior 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition fails to outperform the unconditional 5-day return distribution in at least 55% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in RSAFS indicates stronger household and business spending, which improves revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising RSAFS do not deliver higher 20-day forward returns than falling RSAFS periods in out-of-sample evaluation, or if the effect reverses for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST is also improving, firms face stronger real activity and construction-linked demand, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 3-month average and HOUST is above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-INDPRO/high-HOUST state does not produce positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if its average return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A deterioration in INDPRO without matching demand strength from RSAFS indicates a supply-side slowdown rather than healthy expansion, and that combination lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month average while RSAFS does not rise above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the low-INDPRO/no-RSAFS-improvement state does not predict negative 60-day returns in rolling walk-forward tests, or if the sign is positive in any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness marks a renewed aggregate-demand upswing that supports corporate revenues and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its prior quarterly value after a decline in the previous quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP rebounds fail to coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the average return conditional on a GDP rebound is indistinguishable from zero.
